mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations, as indicated by a 3-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 2.5%, signal central bank tightening pressure that reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through a demand-side multiplier effect.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and INDPRO shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market, indicated by a 3-month decline in UNRATE below 4.5%, signals wage pressures that may force monetary policy restraint, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE's predictive power shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization, boosting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO signal shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or the 5-day forward returns in high INDPRO regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units signals strong consumer confidence and demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through a construction and real estate sector multiplier effect.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or HOUST's predictive power shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong labor market performance and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through a labor-demand feedback loop.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and RSAFS shows no significant correlation with SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 2.0%, raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or FEDFUNDS's predictive power shows no statistical significance (p > 0.1).
